quick_facts.label: Current recruitment check
quick_facts.value: CEPTAM-11’s January 2026 application announcement concerned STA-B and Technician-A posts, not Security Assistant ‘A’. Applicants should rely on DRDO’s live vacancies and CEPTAM notices for any future Security Assistant opening.

sections.heading: Entry route: apply when CEPTAM advertises the post
paragraphs: The direct entry route evidenced by the CEPTAM-10/A&A notice was an online application for Security Assistant ‘A’, followed by Tier-I CBT and, for candidates taken forward, a qualifying Tier-II Physical Fitness and Capability Test. The Tier-I score was used for provisional selection; Tier-II was qualifying in nature.

For the 2022 cycle, the education requirement was 12th class or equivalent, with the stated Armed Forces-certificate alternative for eligible ex-servicemen. Age limits, vacancies, reservation, fee, dates, test standards and posting locations are recruitment-cycle-specific, so they must be read from the next official Security Assistant notice rather than copied from an old advertisement.

As of the cited CEPTAM-11 announcement, the advertised posts were STA-B and Technician-A. Therefore, prospective applicants should monitor DRDO’s vacancies page and CEPTAM notices rather than presume that Security Assistant recruitment is continuously open.
bullets: Complete Class 12 or an accepted equivalent qualification.
Watch for an official CEPTAM advertisement that specifically includes Security Assistant ‘A’.
Apply only through the official process in the applicable notification.
Prepare for both the CBT and the mandatory physical test if the future notice retains the earlier structure.
